pages.title: Sample
pages.summary: Start with the broad sample context. Published repository workflows appear later as example backends rather than at the start of the wizard.

questions.label: What type of sample are you working with?
questions.description: Choose the broad context first.

options.label: Environmental sample
options.help: Environmental surveillance material such as air, passive water, or eDNA.

options.label: Clinical / isolate
options.help: Purified isolates, barcoded isolate collections, or clinical metagenomes.

options.label: Food safety
options.help: Food-associated enrichment and recovery workflows.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Return when the broad sample context is clearer.

pages.title: Material
pages.summary: Describe the biological material or assay class, not the kit. This keeps the early routing generic and reduces repo-specific bias.

questions.label: What kind of material or assay does this represent?
questions.description: Choose the assay branch that best matches your data.

options.label: Long-read metagenomic DNA
options.help: Environmental shotgun DNA for taxonomic, functional, assembly, or AMR analysis.

options.label: RNA virome material
options.help: Environmental RNA material aimed at untargeted viral discovery.

options.label: eDNA 12S amplicon
options.help: Amplicon-based 12S metabarcoding rather than shotgun metagenomics.

options.label: Single isolate long-read DNA
options.help: Single-isolate long-read sequencing aimed at assembly or AMR interpretation.

options.label: Barcoded isolate long-read DNA
options.help: Multiplexed isolate sequencing where one organism is expected per barcode.

options.label: Clinical metagenome
options.help: Complex clinical material rather than a purified isolate.

options.label: Listeria enrichment / recovery material
options.help: Food-safety material intended for Listeria target recovery and comparison workflows.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Return when the assay class is clearer.

pages.title: Target
pages.summary: Choose the main analytical target or deliverable. This is still a generic research question page, not an example-backend page.

questions.label: What do you want to do with the data?
questions.description: Choose one main target or a full-pipeline outcome.

options.label: Taxonomy profiling
options.help: Identify which organisms are present.

options.label: Functional profiling
options.help: Interpret gene content and functional potential.

options.label: MAG recovery
options.help: Recover metagenome-assembled genomes.

options.label: AMR surveillance
options.help: Screen for pathogen and AMR signals in environmental DNA.

options.label: Full environmental pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest environmental DNA action sheet.

options.label: Viral discovery
options.help: Characterize untargeted RNA viral content.

options.label: Full virome pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest RNA virome action sheet.

options.label: Biodiversity metabarcoding
options.help: Use 12S reads for biodiversity inference.

options.label: Host-range inference
options.help: Use 12S reads to infer likely host context from the sample.

options.label: Full amplicon pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest 12S amplicon action sheet.

options.label: Complete genome assembly
options.help: Aim for a high-quality isolate assembly.

options.label: Annotation
options.help: Annotate isolate genome content after assembly.

options.label: Hybrid assembly
options.help: Combine long reads with short reads for isolate assembly.

options.label: AMR / virulence profiling
options.help: Profile AMR and virulence determinants.

options.label: Full isolate pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest isolate action sheet for the chosen route.

options.label: Genome assembly + annotation
options.help: Assemble and annotate barcoded isolates.

options.label: Phylogenetics / outbreak tracking
options.help: Comparative isolate analysis and relatedness.

options.label: Plasmid reconstruction
options.help: Resolve and compare plasmid content.

options.label: AMR host association
options.help: Link AMR genes or plasmids to hosts in a clinical metagenome.

options.label: Full host-association pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest AMR host-association action sheet.

options.label: Listeria target recovery
options.help: Recover and analyze Listeria targets.

options.label: AS vs native comparison
options.help: Compare adaptive sampling against the matched control route.

options.label: Recovered target AMR profiling
options.help: Interpret AMR content after target recovery.

options.label: Full food safety pipeline
options.help: Receive the broadest food-safety action sheet.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Return when the main target is clearer.

pages.title: Published Example Context
pages.summary: Choose the published study context that is closest to your case. Unsupported contexts remain available and map to the nearest published example backend.

questions.label: Which published study context is closest to your case?
questions.description: These options only appear after the generic route is narrowed.

pages.title: Nanopore Setup Advisor
pages.summary: Your route is now defined. Next, refine the sequencing setup and analysis environment without changing the selected published backend.

pages.title: Library Prep Kit
pages.summary: Choose the closest library prep kit or run mode. This mainly changes demultiplexing, trimming, throughput, and how the result frames the early preprocessing steps.

questions.label: Which library prep kit or run mode is closest to your case?
questions.description: The card consequences stay short on purpose: demultiplexing, barcode trimming, typical use, and route implication.

options.label: LSK114
options.help: Ligation-based route focused on read length and single-sample yield.

options.label: RBK114.24
options.help: Rapid barcoding for multiplexed long-read DNA workflows.

options.label: NBD114.24
options.help: Native barcoding for multiplexed long-read DNA workflows.

options.label: Rapid RNA or cDNA workflow
options.help: cDNA-oriented RNA workflow rather than native direct RNA.

options.label: Direct RNA
options.help: Native RNA sequencing rather than cDNA conversion.

options.label: Amplicon workflow
options.help: Standard ONT-style amplicon workflow for 12S products.

options.label: Rapid PCR amplicon
options.help: PCR-biased rapid route for low-input or targeted amplicons.

options.label: Barcoded metagenome
options.help: Multiplexed metagenomic route rather than isolate-only processing.

options.label: Adaptive sampling
options.help: Real-time enrichment or depletion during sequencing.

options.label: Standard control
options.help: Matched non-adaptive sequencing control.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Use the route default and adjust later if needed.

pages.title: Flow Cell
pages.summary: Choose the flow cell family. This tunes throughput framing, compatibility notes, and where the selector suggests Flongle, MinION/GridION, PromethION, or RNA-specific paths.

questions.label: Which flow cell family are you using?
questions.description: Keep this at the family level rather than a full catalogue number.

options.label: Flongle
options.help: Low-output pilot and targeted runs.

options.label: MinION or GridION R10.4.1
options.help: Current general-purpose ONT DNA route.

options.label: PromethION R10.4.1
options.help: High-output ONT route for deeper runs or larger cohorts.

options.label: RNA flow cell
options.help: Direct RNA-specific flow cell family.

options.label: Legacy R9.4.1
options.help: Compatibility route for older workflows or archived runs.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Use the route default and adjust later if needed.

pages.title: Basecalling
pages.summary: Choose the basecalling intent. This determines whether the selector suggests live monitoring, balanced production settings, maximum-accuracy consensus, or no upstream basecalling step at all.

questions.label: What is your basecalling goal?
questions.description: This keeps the selector sparse while still distinguishing FAST, HAC, SUP, modified-base, and already-basecalled routes.

options.label: FAST for real-time monitoring
options.help: Prioritize rapid monitoring, triage, and field-style turnaround.

options.label: Balanced HAC
options.help: Use high accuracy as the general-purpose default.

options.label: Maximum-accuracy SUP
options.help: Favor assembly, AMR, and high-confidence consensus.

options.label: Modified-base calling
options.help: Keep modification-aware basecalling in scope when it is biologically relevant.

options.label: Already basecalled
options.help: Skip upstream basecalling suggestions and start from the documented entry format.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Use the route default and adjust later if needed.

pages.title: Analysis Environment
pages.summary: Choose whether the recommendation should stay fully inside the group repository or also surface ONT-curated and cloud-based fallback environments.

questions.label: Where should the analysis primarily happen?
questions.description: Unsupported routes default to a hybrid reference view: nearest internal example plus external fallback options.

options.label: Group repo backend
options.help: Stay primarily inside the published workflows in this repository.

options.label: EPI2ME Labs
options.help: Use ONT-curated Nextflow workflows as the main analysis environment.

options.label: CZ ID
options.help: Use cloud-based metagenomic interpretation where that is a better fit.

options.label: Hybrid reference
options.help: Show the nearest internal example together with external workflow options.

options.label: Not sure
options.help: Use the route default and adjust later if needed.

pages.title: Conditions
pages.summary: Only a few route-specific conditions appear here. Use them to adjust warnings, preprocessing emphasis, and a small number of heuristics such as short-read metagenomic assembly.

questions.label: Median read length below 1000 bp
questions.description: Use this to switch the preferred environmental assembly heuristic toward nanoMDBG.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Most reads are short for a long-read metagenomic run.

options.label: No
options.help: Read lengths are comfortably above that threshold.

questions.label: Very low-biomass sample
questions.description: Adds more conservative interpretation notes for sparse environmental material.

options.label: Yes
options.help: The sample is sparse or low-yield.

options.label: No
options.help: The sample is not unusually sparse.

questions.label: Assembly is the main deliverable
questions.description: Use this to emphasize assembly-centered interpretation over quick screening.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Recovered genomes matter most.

options.label: No
options.help: Assembly is not the main endpoint.

questions.label: Low viral load expected
questions.description: Adds conservative notes for sparse environmental RNA virome material.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Viral abundance is likely low.

options.label: No
options.help: Viral abundance is not expected to be especially low.

questions.label: Contigs already available
questions.description: Use this to suppress redundant assembly emphasis when contigs already exist.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Contigs already exist.

options.label: No
options.help: Assembly still needs to happen.

questions.label: Barcoded amplicon pool
questions.description: Adds a reminder that pooled amplicons need barcode-aware handling.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Multiple amplicon samples are pooled.

options.label: No
options.help: This is not a pooled amplicon set.

questions.label: Illumina support reads present
questions.description: Adds a short note that short-read support may be used for cross-checking the amplicon interpretation.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Short-read support data are available.

options.label: No
options.help: Only the ONT amplicon data are available.

questions.label: Short reads available for hybrid assembly
questions.description: Use this to foreground hybrid assembly when Illumina support is available.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Short-read support is available.

options.label: No
options.help: Only long reads are available.

questions.label: Closed genome is the priority
questions.description: Adds stronger guidance toward circularization and complete-genome framing.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Closed chromosomes or plasmids matter most.

options.label: No
options.help: A draft genome is sufficient.

questions.label: Per-barcode isolates not yet split
questions.description: Adds stronger demultiplexing and barcode bookkeeping emphasis for multiplexed isolates.

options.label: Yes
options.help: The pooled isolate set still needs separation.

options.label: No
options.help: Per-barcode inputs already exist.

questions.label: Comparative phylogeny is the priority
questions.description: Adds stronger interpretation notes for comparative isolate outputs.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Comparative relatedness matters most.

options.label: No
options.help: The focus is not primarily comparative phylogeny.

questions.label: Host association is the primary deliverable
questions.description: Confirms that linking AMR or plasmids to hosts is the main endpoint.

options.label: Yes
options.help: Host association is the main objective.

options.label: No
options.help: Host association is not the only deliverable.

questions.label: Matched control available
questions.description: Adds stronger adaptive-sampling comparison framing when the control is available.

options.label: Yes
options.help: A matched non-adaptive comparison exists.

options.label: No
options.help: No matched control is available.

questions.label: Target reads already enriched
questions.description: Use this when enrichment has already occurred and the result should focus on downstream comparison and target analysis.

options.label: Yes
options.help: The target fraction is already enriched.

options.label: No
options.help: Further enrichment-sensitive interpretation is still needed.

pages.title: Results
pages.summary: Review the generalized route, the selected published backend, and the Nanopore setup recommendation.
